     The Numbers Business Report

Ant-Man and the Wasp (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $216,648,740Details
International Box Office $406,495,920
Worldwide Box Office $623,144,660
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,194,836
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $33,786,492
Total $50,981,328
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the aftermath of “Captain America: Civil War,” Scott Lang grapples with the consequences of his choices as both a Super Hero and a father. As he struggles to re-balance his home life with his responsibilities as Ant-Man, he’s confronted by Hope van Dyne and Dr. Hank Pym with an urgent new mission. Scott must once again put on the suit and learn to fight alongside The Wasp as the team works together to uncover secrets from their past.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$75,812,205 (35.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.86 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:34.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$130,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,206 opening theaters/4,206 max. theaters, 5.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $284,425,783

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 6th, 2018 (Wide) by Walt Disney
July 6th, 2018 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: July 3rd, 2018 (Wide) (South Korea)
July 5th, 2018 (Wide), released as ЧЕЛОВЕК-МУРАВЕЙ И ОСА (Russia (CIS))
July 6th, 2018 (Wide) (New Zealand)
July 6th, 2018 (Wide), released as Ant-Man a Wasp (Slovakia)
July 6th, 2018 (Wide), released as Skruzdėliukas ir Vapsva (Lithuania)
Video Release: October 2nd, 2018 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some sci-fi action violence.
(Rating bulletin 2532 (Cert #51623), 6/19/2018)
Running Time: 118 minutes
Franchise: Ant-Man, Marvel Cinematic Universe
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Intertitle, Motion Capture Performance, Prologue

Subgenre: Action Adventure

Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Marvel Studios
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In the aftermath of “Captain America: Civil War,” Scott Lang grapples with the consequences of his choices as both a Super Hero and a father. As he struggles to re-balance his home life with his responsibilities as Ant-Man, he’s confronted by Hope van Dyne and Dr. Hank Pym with an urgent new mission. Scott must once again put on the suit and learn to fight alongside The Wasp as the team works together to uncover secrets from their past.
